title: Jonathan Ferguson

Please improve this article by adding [secondary or tertiary sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research#Primary,_secondary_and_tertiary_sources "Wikipedia:No original research").  *Find sources:* ["Jonathan Ferguson"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*
* Historian * author
British

* [Exeter University](/wiki/Exeter_University "Exeter University") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) * [Leicester University](/wiki/Leicester_University "Leicester University") ([PgD](/wiki/Postgraduate_diploma "Postgraduate diploma"))
History of [firearms](/wiki/Firearms "Firearms")

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(born 3 January 1979)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a British [firearm](/wiki/Firearm "Firearm") historian and author who is currently the Keeper of Firearms and [Artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery") at the [Royal Armouries Museum](/wiki/Royal_Armouries_Museum "Royal Armouries Museum") in [Leeds](/wiki/Leeds "Leeds"), England.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) He is also a technical specialist with [Armament Research Services](/wiki/Armament_Research_Services "Armament Research Services"), a consultancy firm.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
["Ferguson, Jonathan, 1979-"](https://id.loc.gov/authorities/names/no2012151863.html). Library of Congress. Retrieved 16 August 2021.
["Gun amnesty: Second Boer War era rifle surrendered in Wales"](https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-wales-49361372). *BBC News*. 17 August 2019. Retrieved 19 May 2021.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British firearm historian (born 1979)

This article is about the historian. For the pilot, see [Jonathan Ferguson (pilot)](/wiki/Jonathan_Ferguson_(pilot) "Jonathan Ferguson (pilot)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies excessively on [references](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") to [primary sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research#Primary,_secondary_and_tertiary_sources "Wikipedia:No original research")**. Please improve this article by adding [secondary or tertiary sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research#Primary,_secondary_and_tertiary_sources "Wikipedia:No original research").  *Find sources:* ["Jonathan Ferguson"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jonathan+Ferguson%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Jonathan Ferguson | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1979-01-03) 3 January 1979 (age 47) |
| Occupation | * Historian * author |
| Nationality | British |
| Education | * [Exeter University](/wiki/Exeter_University "Exeter University") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) * [Leicester University](/wiki/Leicester_University "Leicester University") ([PgD](/wiki/Postgraduate_diploma "Postgraduate diploma")) |
| Subject | History of [firearms](/wiki/Firearms "Firearms") |

**Jonathan Steven Ferguson** (born 3 January 1979)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a British [firearm](/wiki/Firearm "Firearm") historian and author who is currently the Keeper of Firearms and [Artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery") at the [Royal Armouries Museum](/wiki/Royal_Armouries_Museum "Royal Armouries Museum") in [Leeds](/wiki/Leeds "Leeds"), England.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) He is also a technical specialist with [Armament Research Services](/wiki/Armament_Research_Services "Armament Research Services"), a consultancy firm.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jonathan_Ferguson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Ferguson received a [Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts") degree in [archaeology](/wiki/Archaeology "Archaeology") from the [University of Exeter](/wiki/University_of_Exeter "University of Exeter") in 2000, and a [postgraduate diploma](/wiki/Postgraduate_diploma "Postgraduate diploma") in [museum studies](/wiki/Museum_Studies "Museum Studies") from the [University of Leicester](/wiki/University_of_Leicester "University of Leicester") in 2002. He held posts at the [Colchester](/wiki/Colchester "Colchester") Museum, [Imperial War Museum Duxford](/wiki/Imperial_War_Museum_Duxford "Imperial War Museum Duxford"), and [National War Museum of Scotland](/wiki/National_War_Museum_of_Scotland "National War Museum of Scotland") before joining the Royal Armouries Museum in 2009.[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6) He has appeared in two documentaries: *[Sean Bean](/wiki/Sean_Bean "Sean Bean")* on *Waterloo* (2015) and *Sword, Musket & Machine Gun* (2017).[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6) He was also an interview subject in the mockumentary *[Cunk on Earth](/wiki/Cunk_on_Earth "Cunk on Earth")* where he was interviewed as an expert on the [Colt Single Action Army](/wiki/Colt_Single_Action_Army "Colt Single Action Army") revolver. Ferguson is also a Technical Specialist with Armament Research Services, a technical intelligence consultancy, as well as associate editor of the Armax journal of contemporary arms.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

From 2020 to November 2024,[[8]](#cite_note-8) Ferguson appeared on the *[GameSpot](/wiki/GameSpot "GameSpot")* YouTube series titled "Firearms Expert Reacts". In the series, Ferguson analyses depictions of firearms from video games such as *[Escape from Tarkov](/wiki/Escape_from_Tarkov "Escape from Tarkov")*,[[9]](#cite_note-9) *[Call of Duty: Black Ops Cold War](/wiki/Call_of_Duty:_Black_Ops_Cold_War "Call of Duty: Black Ops Cold War")*,[[10]](#cite_note-10) *[Insurgency: Sandstorm](/wiki/Insurgency:_Sandstorm "Insurgency: Sandstorm")*,[[11]](#cite_note-11) and *[Cyberpunk 2077](/wiki/Cyberpunk_2077 "Cyberpunk 2077")*,[[12]](#cite_note-12) and compares their design and function against their real-life counterparts using pieces from the Royal Armouries' collection. In 2021, Ferguson began a series on the Royal Armouries' official [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") channel[[13]](#cite_note-13) in which he explains the history and functionality of select firearms from the Armouries.[[14]](#cite_note-14) In 2025, Ferguson and other familiar faces from the 'Experts React'-videos, started their own YouTube channel named EXP,[[15]](#cite_note-15) to continue with more of the same content as they used to make for GameSpot.

## Select bibliography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jonathan_Ferguson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Select bibliography")]

* *Thorneycroft to SA80: British Bullpup Firearms, 1901–2020* (Headstamp Publishing, 2020)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* *Black & Grey: The Illicit Online Trade of Small Arms in Venezuela* ([Armament Research Services](/wiki/Armament_Research_Services "Armament Research Services"), 2020)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* *An Introductory Guide to the Identification of Small Arms, Light Weapons, and Associated Ammunition* ([Small Arms Survey](/wiki/Small_Arms_Survey "Small Arms Survey"), 2018)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* *Arms and Armour of the First World War* ([Royal Armouries](/wiki/Royal_Armouries_Museum "Royal Armouries Museum"), 2018)[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* *[The 'Broomhandle' Mauser](/wiki/The_%27Broomhandle%27_Mauser "The 'Broomhandle' Mauser")* ([Osprey Publishing](/wiki/Osprey_Publishing "Osprey Publishing"), 2017)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* *Raising Red Flags: An Examination of Arms & Munitions in the Ongoing Conflict in Ukraine* (Armament Research Services, 2014)[[23]](#cite_note-23)
